summary: End-to-end test of Aerotron marketing site. Backend: 9/9 pytest cases passed (health, contact CRUD, validation, Resend email_sent=true, persistence, no _id leak). Frontend: all sections render, all required data-testid attributes present, nav/hero CTAs work, empty-form validation toast works, valid submit clears the form (success path), mobile nav toggle works, zero console errors/warnings.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: GET /api/contact is publicly exposed and returns all inquiries (including PII: name/email/phone/message). For a public marketing site this should be protected by an admin auth check or removed from production; currently anyone can enumerate leads.
server.py send_inquiry_email: uses `reply_to` key — Resend Python SDK expects `reply_to` (snake_case) which is correct for v0.8+, confirmed working (email_sent=true). No action needed.

server.py create_contact_inquiry: if Mongo insert fails but email succeeded, the endpoint silently swallows the DB error and still returns 200. Consider logging and at minimum surfacing a warning header so the operator knows persistence failed.

Contact.jsx: no client-side email-format validation — relies on backend 422. Acceptable but could display `detail` array from FastAPI more gracefully (currently `err.response.data.detail` may be an array of validation errors and would render [object Object]).
backend/.env has RESEND_API_KEY committed; ensure this key is rotated before going to production / public repo.
seed_data_creation: Two TEST_ prefixed contact inquiries inserted via backend + one via UI (all prefixed 'TEST_' for easy cleanup in contact_inquiries collection).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py and run via `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v`. Resend is in test-mode so only lokeshver855@gmail.com receives emails. If a future run shows email_sent=false, check RESEND_API_KEY and network egress. GET /api/contact returns list; use it to verify persistence. Frontend is a single-page anchor-nav site — no auth, no routing.
